module_mood_contract: practitioner fluency — 'I know how to test, I know how to learn, I know how to encode'; Risto-flavoured forward-look at close; no sales-copy, no coach-warmth

rules_evaluated.evidence: M6 is the 'encode' phase of test→learn→encode; its mood is practitioner fluency, not contrast-mood. Rule 2's dosage constraint targets contrast-mood modules (M5-style: learn through doing, name after). No contrast-mood exercise follows this lecture in M6's closer area.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
Body teaches work; trainer manages room outside text.

rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 mood_score: 8. Maintainer block names the contract explicitly. Body honors it: P1 lead-in 'there is no prompt to drill at this point' signals register shift; P4 close on intuition-and-taste is Risto-flavoured forward-look, not benediction or sales-copy. No coach-warmth ('trust your taste' variant absent). Close mood_score: 8.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.

rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture closes on 'your intuition' and hands off to the supplementary and next closer-area lectures ('The loop has a name', 'Agents that build agents'). M6 practitioner-fluency mood does not preempt M7 (awe + ownership) or M8 (open question). The field-is-not-converged framing keeps the arc open.

The register-shift teaching moment ('there is no prompt to drill at this point') is explicit in P1 and fires across reasonable persona-skill variation. The Dino worked-example pointer in P3 grounds the abstract field walk; neither a skip nor a persona-speed difference bypasses it. Trace: persona_action names the register-shift as noticed and absorbed.

M6 Big Idea from strategy doc: 'Encoding is the third phase of test→learn→encode; what two runs taught, packaged so future runs inherit it.' The lecture names the shift from exercise-mode to practitioner-mode composition and points at the supplementary field walk — consistent with the encoding posture. No competing framing introduced.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc: close hands off to next module's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture is in M6's closer area; next is 'The loop has a name' and then 'Agents that build agents' (the final closer pair per maintainer block). P4 close ('one level higher in abstraction... your Claude Code can build the kit you need') hands forward without resolving M7/M8. Trace close.mood_note confirms this.
